choices.text: Multiple edges from one node + merge node. graph.add_edge('start', 'a'); graph.add_edge('start', 'b'); graph.add_edge('start', 'c'); each runs in parallel; merge waits for all. For dynamic count: Send objects via conditional edge. State reducers handle concurrent updates to same field.

questions.explanation: Multiple edges + merge node. Send for dynamic count.

questions.prompt: Need to process 100 documents through same analysis pipeline. Architecture?

choices.text: Map-reduce with Send. Planner node creates Send list (one per document); dispatched to worker node (runs in parallel for all 100); aggregator collects results. 100x faster than sequential. State reducer (append list) collects worker outputs into single results list. For: bulk processing, parallel analysis, scalable document handling.

questions.explanation: Map-reduce with Send. Parallel workers; aggregator merges.
